mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 3.7% signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising unemployment rate are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the housing market index above 1200 signals a strengthening housing market that boosts consumer confidence and spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising housing market index are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production signals a weakening supply chain that reduces corporate profitability and investment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with declining industrial production are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real disposable personal income above 2% signals a strengthening consumer economy that boosts spending and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising real disposable personal income are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in initial unemployment claims above 250,000 signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising initial unemployment claims are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the consumer price index above 2.5% signals a strengthening inflationary environment that reduces purchasing power and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising consumer price index are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the housing market index above 1200 and a 3-month increase in real disposable personal income above 2% signals a strengthening housing market and consumer economy that boosts spending and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising housing market index and real disposable personal income are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
